communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Tennessee Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records pertaining to Wolverine Tube Inc. located at 30500 Railroad Street in Ardmore, Tennessee (Division of Remediation Site ID numbers 28502 and 28511). The facility was listed on the Voluntary Cleanup Program Inventory (VCP), State Remediation Program List (SRP), and Toxics Release Inventory System (TRIS) databases. In my last communication with the TDEC, they requested that I choose from a list of records (attached) to narrow the scope of my request. The desired records are highlighted in yellow. In lieu of OneDrive, I ask that TDEC personnel send responsive records via email to cdowney@augustmack.com. Records may also be photocopied and sent via postal mail.

Kent Hoover, a citizen of Tennessee, in coordination with Caitlin Downey.

communications.subject: Re: Tennessee Public Records Act Request: Tennessee Tech - Tobin Anderson
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

The Tennessee Public Records Act, TCA §10-7-503(a)(2)(A) limits access to public records to Tennessee citizens. Any Tennessee resident can request records made or received pursuant to law or in connection with the transaction of official Tennessee Tech business. Proof of Tennessee citizenship by presentation of a valid Tennessee driver's license (or alternative acceptable form of ID listed at https://www.tntech.edu/ocm/public-records.php) is required as a condition to inspect or receive copies of public records.

If you can reply with an image of your TN ID attached to the email, I can start the process of seeing what records we have in response to your request.

Thank you,


Megan Trotter

Communications Coordinator



Tennessee Tech University

Office of Communications and Marketing

P: 931-372-6755

E: mtrotter@tntech.edu<mailto:mtrotter@tntech.edu>

title: ERO DEN Ceren Detention Records

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: ERO DEN Ceren Detention Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All records of communication reflecting the decision by ICE headquarters, ICE's Office of Principal Legal Advisor, and the Enforcement and Removal Operations Denver Field Office agents to continue the detention of Hugo Alexander Hernandez Ceren, A 073 956 722, following his arrest on November 24, 2025, during his voluntary report to a Los Angeles ICE office pursuant to an Order of Supervision.
2. All records pertaining to decisions by ICE HQ, OPLA, and/or ICE ERO Denver regarding Mr. Hernandez Ceren's transportation and detention in the Aurora ICE Processing Center. 
3. All records pertaining to any review  ICE HQ, OPLA, HQPDU, and/or ICE ERO Denver conducted regarding the legality of his detention post-November 24, 2025. 
4. All records of any communication between ICE and The GEO Group regarding Mr. Hernandez Ceren. 

Pursuant to 6 C.F.R. 5.5(e), I request expedited processing of this request and state that the following is true and correct to the best of my knowledge under penalty of perjury pursuant to 28 USC 1746: 

1. I am a member of the news media, primarily engaged in information dissemination. I write edited articles for local and national outlets. My bylines are available here: www.MuckRack.Com/Andrew-Free. I am a member of Investigative Reporters and Editors, a professional association open only to journalists. I also publish a frequently cited blog focused on transparency, suffering, and harm in ICE's detention system. 
2. There is an urgent need to inform the public about the process by which ICE ERO Los Angeles decided to re-detain Mr. Hernandez Ceren. 
3. On March 12, 2026, the U.S. District Court for the District of Colorado concluded his detention was unlawful and ordered his immediate release.  Hernandez Ceren v. Noem, Case No. 1:26-cv-00560-RMR (D. Colo. Mar. 12, 2026). 
4. On December 8, 2025, Mr. Hernandez Ceren's deportation was halted by an order Judge John Kane, U.S. District Court for the District of Colorado, in Menocal v. The GEO Group, Inc., No. 1:14-cv-02887 (D. Colo.), making his deportation in the reasonably foreseeable future legally impossible. 
5. Prior to Mr. Hernandez Ceren's re-detention on November 24, 2025, Judge Kane a judicial U-Visa Certification affirming that he was a prima facie victim a qualifying crime, that he had assisted authorities in investigating that crime, and that his presence in the U.S. was necessary. 
6. The underlying offense upon which Judge Kane based his U-Visa certification was forced labor, 18 USC 1589. 
7. The offender allegedly committing the offense was The GEO Group, ICE's largest detention contractor. 
8. Mr. Hernandez Ceren is a named plaintiff and class representative in the Menocal case, which GEO recently lost at the Supreme Court. 
9. As a result of his unlawful re-detention, Mr. Hernandez Ceren was unable to attend the biometrics appointment for his pending U visa, which may force him to re-apply. 
10. As a result of unlawful re-detention, Mr. Hernandez Ceren was subjected to additional labor conditions that violate the TVPA in GEO's Adelanto and Aurora facilities. 
11. ICE and GEO's unlawful labor practices have been the subject of widespread media attention about which there exist questions as to the government's conduct. 
12. Mr. Hernandez Ceren's participation in the Menocal case appears likely to have occurred as a direct result of his participation in the forced labor case against GEO, based on the statements of ERO officers to Mr. Hernandez Ceren.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Andrew Free

title: USS Liberty: Processing of June 8, 2005 War Crimes Report

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: USS Liberty: Processing of June 8, 2005 War Crimes Report
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552, I hereby request copies of all records, including but not limited to internal memoranda, legal opinions, routing slips (Form DD-173 or equivalent), and email correspondence, regarding the receipt and processing of the document titled "A Report: War Crimes Committed Against U.S. Military Personnel, June 8, 1967," which was filed with the Secretary of the Army on June 8, 2005. and is available for download at https://www.documentcloud.org/documents/22135684-wcr-with-exhibits/.

Specifically, I request:

    All Action Memoranda or Information Memoranda generated by the Office of the Secretary of the Army (OSA) or the Army’s Executive Agent for the Law of War Program regarding this filing between June 2005 and June 2007.

    All Legal Reviews or Opinions conducted by the Army Office of the Judge Advocate General (OTJAG) or the DoD Office of General Counsel concerning the applicability of DoD Directive 5100.77 to the allegations contained in the June 8, 2005 report.

    Any correspondence between the Office of the Secretary of the Army and the Department of the Navy or the Office of the Secretary of Defense regarding the determination not to initiate a formal investigation into the reported incidents.

Fee Waiver Request: This request is made on behalf of the USS Liberty Veterans Association, a non-profit veterans' organization. The records are sought to inform the public and survivors regarding the government's adherence to its own war crimes reporting directives. I request a waiver of all fees.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Joe Meadors
